fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enables normal firmware-visible controller operation.

fields.name: SOFT_RESET
fields.description: Write 1 to request a software-visible reset action; the bit self-clears after being sampled and cleared state returns to zero.

fields.name: IRQ_ENABLE
fields.description: Enables irq assertion when BIST done or fail status is latched.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

fields.name: ready
fields.description: Controller is idle and accepting MMIO activity.

fields.name: bist_done
fields.description: Latched BIST completion indicator.

fields.name: bist_fail
fields.description: Latched BIST failure indicator.

fields.name: busy
fields.description: Controller is busy running BIST or otherwise not ready.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

fields.name: memory_address
fields.description: Word address used for subsequent SRAM read or write requests; upper bits are ignored.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

fields.name: memory_write_data
fields.description: Write data presented to the SRAM interface on a write request.

fields.name: memory_read_data
fields.description: Last observed SRAM read data or the current synchronous read result as implemented by the memory model.

fields.name: MEM_WRITE
fields.description: One-cycle sampled request to perform a single-port SRAM write using MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA.

fields.name: MEM_READ
fields.description: One-cycle sampled request to perform a single-port SRAM read using MEM_ADDR.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

registers.name: BIST_CONTROL
registers.description: BIST request and result-clear control register.

fields.name: START
fields.description: Requests BIST. Logically ORed with the external bist_start input.

fields.name: CLEAR_RESULT
fields.description: Clears latched BIST done/fail status, IRQ status, and last_fail_addr.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

registers.name: BIST_STATUS
registers.description: Latched BIST state and last failing address.

fields.name: done
fields.description: BIST completed successfully or reached terminal completion state.

fields.name: fail
fields.description: BIST detected a failure condition.

fields.name: running
fields.description: BIST is currently active.

fields.name: last_fail_addr
fields.description: Address of the last failing SRAM word, 6 bits wide.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

fields.name: bist_done
fields.description: Latched BIST-done interrupt cause.

fields.name: bist_fail
fields.description: Latched BIST-fail interrupt cause.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved, reads as zero.

fields.name: clear_done
fields.description: Write 1 to clear the latched BIST-done interrupt cause.

fields.name: clear_fail
fields.description: Write 1 to clear the latched BIST-fail interrupt cause.

fields.name: RSVD
fields.description: Reserved; writes have no effect.

sources.name: bist_done
sources.description: Asserted via irq when IRQ_ENABLE is set and BIST done status is latched.

sources.name: bist_fail
sources.description: Asserted via irq when IRQ_ENABLE is set and BIST fail status is latched.

init_sequence: Deassert reset and wait until STATUS.ready becomes 1.
Optionally write CONTROL.ENABLE=1 to enable normal operations.
Optionally write CONTROL.IRQ_ENABLE=1 if interrupt-driven BIST completion reporting is desired.
Write MEM_ADDR with the target 6-bit SRAM word address.
Write MEM_WDATA with the data payload for a write transaction, then set MEM_CONTROL.MEM_WRITE=1 for one cycle and clear it if software uses level-sensitive writes.
For a read transaction, write MEM_ADDR then set MEM_CONTROL.MEM_READ=1 for one cycle and read back MEM_RDATA.
polling_sequence: Poll STATUS.busy until 0 if a BIST or other busy condition is expected.
Poll BIST_STATUS.done and BIST_STATUS.fail to observe completion state.
Read IRQ_STATUS to determine which BIST cause is latched when irq is asserted.
irq_sequence: Start BIST by writing BIST_CONTROL.START=1 or asserting bist_start.
Read BIST_STATUS and IRQ_STATUS to determine done versus fail and last_fail_addr.
Clear interrupt causes by writing IRQ_CLEAR with the appropriate write-one-to-clear bits set, or clear all BIST result state using BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT.

consistency_notes: All register addresses, field positions, and semantics are compatible with DIGITAL_SPEC_JSON.
The register map is kept on a 32-bit data model because the source specification defines 32-bit MMIO inputs and outputs.
No new hierarchy, ports, or bus protocol are introduced.
